treatment_information.dosage_or_details: coronal approach, temporary zygomatic arch osteotomy, muscular stripping and stretching, forced mouth opening, repositioning and plating of the arch, resection of the lower border of the enlarged malar body, suction drain
treatment_information.treatment_response: mouth opening of 30 mm achieved, enabling normal diet and dental care
mouth opening of 35 mm after 18-month follow-up

treatment_timeline.event: Patient treated for TMJ arthrosis with masseter muscle weakening surgery, unsuccessful
Patient referred to unit, diagnosed with unilateral right coronoid hyperplasia
Surgery performed: coronoidectomy with fasciotomy and myotomy
Mouth opening of 30 mm achieved, enabling normal diet and dental care
Mouth opening of 35 mm after 18-month follow-up

patient_outcome.final_status: improved mouth opening (35 mm), able to consume normal diet and take care of teeth
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: intensive follow-up, 18-month follow-up showed continued improvement
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Early surgical treatment may be useful to recover morphology and function, but severity of mandibular asymmetry will be revaluated at age 6 to 8 years
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Mouth opening improved from 5 mm to 30 mm post-surgery, and to 35 mm after 18 months
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: able to consume normal diet and take care of teeth
